title: Peschiera muricata
intro: Peschiera muricata, also known as Bael, is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia and a member of the Rutaceae family, characterized by its distinctive white flowers and edible fruits. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Peschiera muricata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Peschiera muricata is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to promote overall health and well-being.
